### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03029-email`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Stamp & Shape Email

### Stamp & Shape Email

Shapes the item: writes `messageId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.messageId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Classify with AI · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Urgent, Routine, FYI (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Urgent · ②→ Store as Routine, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Store as FYI · ④→ Alert Slack — Other

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

# Digest meeting notes with Google Sheets and Discord for your team

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Digest meeting notes with Google Sheets and Discord for your team. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual auto-responders is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Stamp & Shape Email → Payload Valid? → Classify with AI → Store as Urgent → Reunite Branches → Load Previous Snapshot → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating auto-responders in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Standardizes the process every time
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your auto-responders process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
